title: Hieracium lindebergii
intro: Hieracium lindebergii, commonly known as Lindeberg's hawkweed, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Hieracium lindebergii is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hieracium lindebergii is typically harvested for use in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support joint health and immune function.
taxonomy: Hieracium lindebergii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Hieracium, commonly known as hawkweeds. Within the Asteraceae family, Hieracium lindebergii is part of the subfamily Cichorioideae. Further classification places it within the tribe Cichorieae. Its species classification is within the section Lindebergioides.
